moveAnnotations.annotation: We're playing the Sodium Attack, an unusual and provocative first move that immediately challenges conventional opening theory. By placing our knight on the rim (a3), we're signaling that we don't intend to fight for the center immediately—instead, we'll reroute it to better squares like c4 or b5 while keeping our opponent guessing. This move is rarely seen at high levels because it cedes space and tempo, but it can catch unprepared opponents off-guard and lead to interesting, imbalanced positions where creative play matters more than theoretical knowledge. The key is to follow up intelligently with a coherent plan, either supporting a central advance later or launching an early queenside attack.
